# RFC-0007: Runtime Image Production Contract

> **Version:** 0.2.0 | **Status:** normative | **Phase:** test

---

## 1. Summary

### [RFC-0007:C-SUMMARY] Summary (Informative) <a id="rfc-0007c-summary"></a>

This RFC governs runtime image production: building validated OCI images from one named stack, content-closure reuse, recipe-and-server-case validation, local placement eligibility, and artifact landing. [RFC-0001](../rfc/RFC-0001.md) holds the specification overview and authority map.

*Since: v0.1.0*

---

## 2. Specification

### [RFC-0007:C-IMAGE-BUILD] Runtime Image Production Boundary (Normative) <a id="rfc-0007c-image-build"></a>

Inferlab MUST support runtime-image production as a workflow separate from the long-running server and closed-loop recipe lifecycles.

An image definition MUST identify one reproducible runtime-image unit with exactly one `stack`, one `base_image`, one or more `platforms`, optional `packages` drawn only from that stack's `source_paths`, and zero or more `validations`. When the package subset is omitted, every stack `source_paths` entry is selected. A stack with no source paths therefore supports only an empty local-package selection.

Each validation MUST name one `recipe` and MAY name `server_case` for that recipe's server. When `server_case` is omitted, the server's normal case-selection rule applies. The referenced server stack MUST equal the image stack. A validation MUST NOT restate server, placement, model locator, or measurement configuration.

Runtime-image production MUST use the selected stack, workspace source state, Pixi lock, integration selection, local builder and placement bindings, and evidence facilities defined by this specification where those facts apply.

A runtime-image build MUST reject a dirty workspace.

Every referenced stack, recipe, server, server case, package path, placement, and builder binding MUST be structurally valid before the first state-affecting external command. A missing reference, invalid explicit case, package outside the image stack's source paths, or server/image stack mismatch MUST fail the image build before a record or builder effect.

The public runtime CLI MUST provide `image build <image>` with optional `--builder`, `--placement`, `--export`, `--local`, and `--dry-run`. It MUST execute resolution, assembly, inspection, requested export, and eligible validations as one closed-loop workflow.

A non-dry-run `image build` MUST report the record identity on the operator diagnostic stream as soon as the record exists, and MUST report each package build, assembly, inspection, export, and validation phase as it begins, including the durable log path where one exists. Machine-readable output MUST remain a single final report.

`image build` MUST support dry-run. Dry-run MUST report each target platform, content-closure identity, assembly deduplication plan, resolution observations, selected placement when applicable, and each validation eligibility classification. It MUST NOT assemble, export, launch validation servers, execute stack checks, or create a record claiming execution.

Inferlab MUST generate the image build context from resolved stack facts: the committed Pixi lock, locally built selected packages, and resolved base image. Image production MUST NOT require authored container build files. The frozen context MUST contain only generated build files, selected-stack check and postprocess scripts, and packaged artifacts; package scratch, sanitized source copies, and durable logs MUST remain outside. Failure to remove sanitized copies MUST fail assembly and be preserved even when another failure is primary.

Image resolution MUST resolve the declared base image to an immutable per-platform image digest before assembly.

Image resolution MUST scope the assembly plan to the declared target platforms the selected builder can produce. An unproducible platform MUST be excluded before its content closure is derived and MUST be reported with a reason in dry-run output, the execution record, and the product manifest; exclusion is not a failure. When the selected builder can produce none of the declared platforms, resolution MUST fail.

Every `packages` entry MUST equal one path in the image stack's `source_paths`. Source paths consumed only at build time remain inputs without producing their own wheel.

Before building any required package, a non-dry-run image build MUST execute the selected stack checks against its local Pixi realization ([RFC-0002:C-ENVIRONMENT-CHECKS](../rfc/RFC-0002.md#rfc-0002c-environment-checks)). Failure MUST abort before package build, present the repair hint for a check verdict, finalize the record, and still emit the single failed machine-readable report.

Runtime-image production MUST build required packages from the resolved stack source state and fail before assembly when one cannot be produced. Reuse is allowed only when committed source identity, the stack's Pixi environment locked closure, editable-package content outside selected source paths, projected activation, generator and build-procedure identity, and target platform are unchanged. Cache publication MUST be private-staged, atomic, and immutable; a race adopts the existing artifact. Built package content digests and logs are execution evidence, and package payloads MUST NOT remain in a successful build context.

A locally built package MUST replace the identically named locked upstream package in the image content, so an image never mixes locally built sources with their lagging upstream releases.

Package builds MUST run in a clean process environment — only composed activation, a fixed system-utility path suffix, and sanitized-view redirects reach the build — and MUST use sanitized copies of the selected stack source paths, with build-time source references redirected into that view. An activation value carrying more than one workspace-root reference MUST be rejected rather than partially redirected. Activation projection MUST compose feature-scoped activation according to the selected stack Pixi environment: earlier-listed features take precedence, a feature target table overrides its untargeted table, and the default feature applies last unless the environment excludes it; this precedence MUST be verified against observed Pixi behavior. Activation scripts binding to the selected environment MUST be rejected at image resolution rather than omitted because their exports are not statically knowable; scripts under features the environment does not compose are inert. Inferlab MUST audit workspace source mutation after package builds whether or not the build succeeded, fail assembly while recording mutated paths, preserve the build failure when both occurred, and never clean or revert the workspace. An audit that cannot execute MUST itself fail assembly while preserving the build outcome.

Image assembly MUST execute the selected stack image-realization postprocess steps and then its checks inside the image build, after environment installation, through the image entrypoint ([RFC-0002:C-ENVIRONMENT-CHECKS](../rfc/RFC-0002.md#rfc-0002c-environment-checks)). A failed check MUST fail assembly before an immutable image identity exists, so builder storage, reuse, export, and product manifest never observe an unchecked image. The durable builder log MUST attribute every executed check and outcome.

The resolved image content closure MUST derive from pre-assembly facts: selected stack identity, required package source identity, committed Pixi lock digest and selected Pixi environment, resolved base-image digest, generated entrypoint, selected stack check and postprocess content, generator identity, and package-build and context-generation procedure identities. The package-build procedure identity MUST enter package reuse and closure identity. Built package digests are execution evidence, not closure inputs. The closure MUST NOT incorporate builder hosts, workspace paths, placements, machine bindings, model-weight locators, or validations.

Model weights MUST remain external runtime inputs. An image build MUST NOT copy model weights or model-weight locators into its build context or output.

Builder hosts, machine names, local filesystem paths, and the machine-private facts known to resolution MUST NOT enter generated build-context text, image configuration, or OCI metadata, including image labels and annotations. This exclusion guarantee is scoped to generated text and image metadata: locally built binary payloads are protected by the sanitized-copy build rule rather than by text verification, and MAY embed paths of the sanitized build tree. Generated context text MUST be verified before assembly against the machine-private facts known to resolution and against host home-directory paths; activation values that would escape the entrypoint's quoting MUST be rejected. Activation variables whose names identify credential material MUST be excluded from the projected entrypoint and recorded as excluded; this name-based exclusion is best-effort protection for committed workspace content, whose freedom from secrets remains the workspace author's responsibility. Declared check and postprocess scripts copied into the context are committed workspace content under that same posture. Inferlab does not promise secret scanning of committed manifests, locks, or package URLs. The no-redaction evidence contract of this specification applies to local execution records; portable image artifacts MUST exclude machine-private facts from generated text and metadata by construction.

Local bindings MAY declare container environment pass-through variable names per machine. Declared names MUST be validated when bindings load: a name that is not a POSIX shell identifier, a duplicate, or a name Inferlab itself manages inside the container MUST be rejected — the launch scripts splice these names into shell parameter references, where anything beyond an identifier can carry expansion side effects, and a non-identifier name could not be referenced by the channel at all. A validation container MUST receive each declared variable by name reference only, so its value never enters the launch command line or the image content; a declared variable absent from the launching environment MUST be reported. The local execution record preserves the launch command as executed.

Local bindings MAY also declare per-machine container hardware facts for that same containerized substitution: host device paths, an unlimited pinned-memory ulimit, and Linux capabilities. These facts are operator-declared and MUST NOT be auto-detected. Declarations MUST be validated when bindings load: a device path that is not absolute, a duplicate device, a capability outside the fixed set Inferlab recognizes, or a duplicate capability MUST be rejected. A validation container MUST receive each declared device, the declared ulimit, and each declared capability; a containerized launch MUST NOT request privileged mode. Container hardware facts are runtime launch facts: they MUST NOT enter image content, content closures, or portable artifacts.

Within one `image build` invocation, equal content closure and target platform MUST reuse one assembly even when several validations consume it.

The immutable image identity MUST enter the assembly evidence the moment the builder returns it, so a later cleanup, inspection, or export failure cannot lose which image the assembly produced. Image inspection MUST establish and record the immutable image identity and the entrypoint contract of the built artifact.

Every validation in one invocation MUST use the same placement selected by `--placement` or the normal local placement-selection rule; Inferlab MUST NOT choose a placement per validation. Validation MUST reuse the referenced server and case resolution, readiness, workload-suite measurement, evidence, and cleanup semantics, with the built image substituting for the local stack realization.

A validation is eligible for a target platform only when that platform matches the selected local builder host, the selected placement can realize every server rank on that host without semantic incompatibility, and a model locator resolves for every model-serving rank. Eligible validations run against that platform's assembled image.

An ineligible validation MUST be recorded as built-but-unvalidated with its reason, not failed. A validation whose target-platform assembly failed MUST record failed-assembly, not built-but-unvalidated. Once eligible validation execution begins, a server, readiness, measurement, or cleanup failure MUST record a failed validation.

A failed assembly or validation for one platform or validation MUST NOT prevent other requested platforms or validations from completing.

Builder selection MUST be deterministic. When local bindings declare more than one builder, the invocation MUST select one explicitly.

Built images MUST remain in local builder storage under a recorded immutable image identity by default. An operator MUST be able to request export to a local OCI archive. Each exported archive path MUST be unique to its producing record, so no repeated or concurrent build overwrites another record's artifact evidence. Configured artifact storage MAY receive exported artifacts.

The image-production workflow defined by this specification MUST stop at local or configured artifact storage and a product manifest. The image-production workflow MUST NOT push images to a registry.

The product manifest MUST map each requested platform and validation to image identity, equal-closure deduplication, produced artifacts, validation references, eligibility and execution outcomes, and complete or partial workflow outcome.

Image records MUST preserve build inputs, selected stack and placement, native builder commands, immutable image identity, inspection and export evidence, stack-check outcomes for every realization, validation outcomes, partial failures, and cleanup. Read-only builder-platform and base-image-digest probes MUST be recorded with exact commands in the resolved plan. Every state-affecting builder command MUST be persisted before execution and equal the executed command.

**Rationale:** Multi-platform and multi-model image output is an established product goal, but it does not require runtime serving and recipe execution to share a general operation DAG. A named image definition keeps image production a shareable workspace fact, recipe-referenced validation keeps one authority for server and measurement configuration, and a content closure derived only from generated, digest-pinned inputs makes deduplication verifiable while excluding weights and private facts by construction. Records stay honest locally while the generated text and metadata that leave the machine carry no machine-private facts: evidence is preserved where it is private, and excluded where it is portable. The portability promises are sized to what the tool can actually enforce — fail-closed verification for facts Inferlab knows, best-effort exclusion for committed content, sanitized views plus mutation detection instead of an unenforceable no-mutation absolute — because a guarantee the mechanism cannot honor is worse than a narrower one it can. Clean-only image inputs protect reproducible artifacts, and external weights with content-based reuse keep image outputs portable and avoid redundant assembly. Entry checks price a doomed build at seconds instead of an hour, and in-image checks executed as build steps mean an image that exists has passed its declared invariants — no unverified artifact can be cached, exported, or validated. Declared container hardware facts keep RDMA-dependent serving launchable in containers — pinned memory, InfiniBand and GPUDirect device nodes — while the operator states exactly what each host grants: no auto-detection to guess wrong, no privileged mode to grant everything, and no machine facts leaking into portable artifacts.

*Since: v0.1.0*
